content: Engineers Sam and the DevOps team encountered a critical technical issue where users were randomly logged out across all devices due to a mismatch in JWT signing keys between the API gateway and the authentication service. The root cause was a missed pod restart after the DevOps team rotated the JWT_SECRET_KEY in the environment variables, resulting in the authentication service signing tokens with an outdated key that failed validation in the API gateway. To resolve this issue, a Kubernetes ConfigMap reloader sidecar was implemented, which automatically triggers a rolling restart of pods whenever environment variables or secrets are updated, thereby ensuring that changes to sensitive keys are promptly reflected across the system.

Engineers Priya and her team encountered a technical issue with the GitHub Actions deployment pipeline, which had been failing for four hours on the "Build Image" step due to a dependency resolution conflict. The root cause was identified as a compatibility issue between React v18 and an outdated charting library, which had not been updated to support the newer version of React. To resolve the immediate issue, Priya temporarily added the --legacy-peer-deps flag to the npm install command in the Dockerfile, allowing the build to pass. However, to prevent similar issues in the future, a high-priority Jira ticket was created to migrate off the deprecated charting library before the next major release.
